programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve heads to Zambia for the first time. He hears hippo communication on a whole new level, spots a high-diving croc and meets some incredible sniffer dogs.

programme.title: Minions
programme.desc: Minions Kevin, Stuart and Bob attend the 1968 Villain-Con, where they land the job of sidekicks to supervillain Scarlet Overkill. She tells them of her evil plans to become Queen of England.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: It’s the team’s most confounding puzzle yet when a passenger boards a flight to Dominica but then seemingly vanishes mid-flight, only to be found back on Saint Marie soil... shot dead. The pilot and flight attendant deny ever seeing the victim on their plane, raising the team’s suspicions. Two further suspects are found back in Saint Marie – it transpires the victim had been caught up in a messy love affair. Neville starts to question whether he’s losing his mind, seriously doubting himself for the first time, until all the pieces of the puzzle finally fit together, revealing the startling truth. Meanwhile, an old face has returned, and Neville has a huge decision to make.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: There’s no place like home when Dickie takes his work mates on a Wizard of Oz night out.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Mam is managing a team for a local charity football match, where Dickie and Lucinda compete to become the best wags!

programme.title: Big Proud Party Agency
programme.desc: Best friends Jordan and Tre ask the agents to come up with a birthday bash, where Jordan also plans a surprise proposal to his boyfriend Corey.

programme.title: Big Proud Party Agency
programme.desc: Best friends Sam and James want to throw one last big party with their friend Xav before they all go their separate ways to uni. Which agent will host their last hurrah?

programme.title: Big Proud Party Agency
programme.desc: Best friends Libby and Jess from Southampton want to arrange an unforgettable 18th birthday party for identical twins Holly and Charlotte, who are Libby’s younger sisters. Despite having already turned 18, the twins never got to celebrate in the proper style due to the pandemic, so the girls are hoping the Big Proud Party Agency can put things right. After hearing what the girls have to say, the party agents compete to win the business. Ryan pitches a VIP glam-up disco with high-fashion style and paparazzi. Christopher whets the appetite with a pool party featuring some half-naked 'mermen', and Teddy wants to set sail on a superyacht before ending the night in a super club. After Libby and Jess choose their favourite party idea, it’s time to get down to business. The winning party agent, supported by the others, heads to Southampton to put their plan into action. The décor has to be hand-picked to suit the theme, performers are auditioned for their very specific talents, and a dance routine is lined up as an extra special surprise. Will the twins get the 18th birthday party they always wanted?

programme.title: Big Proud Party Agency
programme.desc: Best friends Levi and Beth from Cardiff want to organise a class prom for their musical theatre year group who graduated without the normal celebrations due to Covid restrictions. They want the event to centre around leading light Geraint, aka Queen G, who has become noticeably introverted after a homophobic attack left him fearing for his life. After getting their head around the brief, the party agents compete to win the business. Ryan tempts Levi and Beth with his High School Musical-inspired Americana prom. Teddy wants the theme of her party to draw on the 1980s underground ballroom scene - complete with male strippers, and Christopher promises a decadent art deco black and gold affair in a stunning country mansion. After Levi and Beth choose their favourite party idea, it’s time to get down to business. The winning party agent, supported by the others, heads to Cardiff to put their plan into action. A bespoke couture outfit is designed, an amazing ornamental centrepiece is crafted from scratch, and the venue’s doors need to be altered to accommodate a giant glitter ball. Will Geraint rediscover his inner Queen G?

programme.title: Iolo's River Valleys
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores the Tywi river valley, from Llansteffan’s coastal dunes to fallow deer ruts, ancient oak woodlands and the remote heights of the Tywi forest.

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: Meticulous and methodical, Big John engineers his ransom scheme with military precision. On paper, it’s flawless. But as the deadline approaches, nothing goes according to plan, and two battles unfold simultaneously: a high-stakes ransom exchange and a desperate race to stop a bomb designed to be unstoppable. Precision planning meets a chaotic reality, and one miscalculation on either side could mean catastrophe.

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: Surgeons face a difficult dilemma in the middle of a life-saving bile duct cancer operation. A chest sarcoma proves more challenging than expected, with no room for error.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Richie Anderson is house-hunting in North Yorkshire with Sarah. who is on the hunt for a beautiful stone cottage in a thriving community. With a budget of £220,000 and support from her son Christian, they explore a range of tempting, characterful homes. Richie also visits a young couple who have embraced rural life on their farm, where they’ve started what they claim is the world’s first egg restaurant.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Let’s hope it’s not all downhill for two teams of bargain hunters as they scour the stalls of an antiques centre in Kingston upon Thames.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo’s railway tour of Sardinia heads inland towards the island's dramatic mountain landscapes, which he discovers were frequented by DH Lawrence, who is revered in this remote spot. Michael visits the spectacular Nuraghe, mysterious Bronze Age conical stone structures unique to Sardinia, where remarkable artefacts have been unearthed to reveal an advanced society of engineers, navigators and craftsmen. Immersing himself in Sardinian traditions, Michael visits a family-run agriturismo, where he learns the art of making delicate ravioli. Finally, he embarks on a nostalgic journey aboard Il Trenino Verde, one of Europe’s longest narrow-gauge railways, which winds through Sardinia’s rugged interior.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael travels to the cultural heart of Japan, Kyoto, the city that for a thousand years was the capital. At the Rozanji temple, he learns about a book that is described as the world’s first novel, the Tale of Genji. Across the city, Michael visits the last remaining wagasa umbrella workshop in Kyoto. At Kyoto’s Matsunoo shrine, Michael gets a beginner’s guide to shrine etiquette and discovers the countless daily Shinto rituals that have become part of Japanese life. The day ends with Michael taking a trip to a local restaurant to meet Satoha, an apprentice geisha, to find out how this ancient artform is adapting to modern life.

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael’s South Korean railway journey continues in Seoul. Travelling across one of the world’s largest metro systems, he heads south to Suwon to visit Samsung’s headquarters, tracing the rise of the family-run company from a small trading business to a global technology giant and symbol of Korea’s postwar economic miracle. Touring a futuristic smart home, Michael explores how innovation now shapes everyday life. Back in central Seoul, he visits the city’s original railway station, built during Japanese rule and now preserved as a cultural space, reflecting the complexities of Korea’s colonial past. In Gangnam, a district propelled to global stardom by a K-pop hit, he explores the international success of Korean popular culture, meeting the boss and dancers at a K-pop academy to understand how Hallyu – the Korean cultural wave – has conquered international audiences. Finally, in Seodaemun, Michael visits a former prison and Independence Park to uncover the story of the March First Movement of 1919, a pivotal moment in Korea’s struggle for independence from Japan, meeting a descendant of a leading resistance figure whose courage helped shape Korea’s struggle for freedom.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards presents the quiz with a third dimension. In traditional quiz shows, questions have just right and wrong answers. But here, there is a twist - the Impossible answer. It's not just wrong, it couldn't possibly be right. Each day, 24 regular contestants compete for a possible £10,000 jackpot. Right answers keep them on track for the big money, but if at any stage they give an Impossible answer, that immediately knocks them out and they must try again the next day. Every show climaxes with the £10,000 question as the daily winner faces one last challenge. Three right answers trigger a spectacular cascade of 10,000 pound coins from a giant exclamation mark, but a single Impossible answer means they will leave with nothing.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: The hunt for the nation's Mastermind continues as Clive Myrie tests four more contenders on their specialist subjects and general knowledge. The specialist subjects are the Peter Wimsey stories of Dorothy L Sayers, Arnold Schwarzenegger, The Wire and the Falklands conflict.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: The Olgun family's rented two-bed flat in south west London is bursting at the seams. Their hallway is a pinch point with outdated storage and clutter in full view, so the team create a bespoke worktop and revamp the tired units to make the space fit for purpose. Then the much-used kitchen and bathroom are deep cleaned, ridding them of grease and limescale.

programme.title: Wonders of Life
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox asks how, from a lifeless cosmos ruled by the laws of physics and chemistry, it is possible that a planet can produce so much wonderful, varied biology.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: For the last programme in the present series Michael Aspel and the experts visit Harrogate in Yorkshire. Among the items they unearth are naval log books belonging to a Captain who escorted Napoleon to St Helena, 'one of the most lovely silk bedcovers' Bunny Campione has ever seen and an 'absolutely gorgeous' carriage clock made for the daughter of a great Victorian philanthropist.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Members of the public try to make money out of their antiques.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Josh Jones, Molly Rainford, Mark Rhodes and Sonia take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his Sardinian railway adventure, arriving in Mamoiada to investigate the town’s extraordinary pagan traditions. In a menacing mask, black sheepskin and 30 kg of cowbells, Michael embraces the role of a mamuthone, who wards off evil spirits. Taking a 1950s diesel railcar, Michael journeys across the island to Macomer, where he discovers the railway’s surprising connection to a pioneering Welsh railway engineer. His adventure concludes at Italy’s largest cork factory, where a team of expert sniffers safeguard the integrity of the world’s finest wines.

programme.title: Iolo's River Valleys
programme.desc: Iolo explores the Teifi river valley and finds a slow worm hunting, meets a coracle fisherman, is thrilled by a fishing otter and watches a pair of kestrels at their nest.

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Can artificial intelligence read emotions better than a human? Hannah explores the new technology that is creeping into our lives.

programme.title: The Secret Genius of Modern Life
programme.desc: The rollercoaster has been sending our hearts racing and stomachs dropping for over a century, and today, millions of us get our kicks on these topsy-turvy, daredevil joyriders. Hannah gets the inside story on the UK’s newest, tallest and fastest rollercoaster – Thorpe Park’s Hyperia. She uncovers the wild origins of the modern-day coaster and finds out how some dodgy French physics in the 19th century, Nasa rocket scientists and an ambitious cartoonist called Walt delivered the ultimate thrill machine.

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Monty Don and Arit Anderson explore how gardening can inspire us to be more creative while celebrating the artistry on display in the showground and Great Pavilion. Rachel de Thame reveals who has been named RHS Florist of the Year 2026, and Adam Frost delves into the latest design trends and explains how you can replicate them at home.

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A surgeon must remove part of his patient’s tongue and reconstruct it to save her from an aggressive cancer, and surgeons perform a rare simultaneous pancreas and kidney transplant.

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: Stephen joins response officers Anna and Perri as they attend a 999 call made from a property in Belfast. A man has had bleach squirted directly on his face and into his eyes during a domestic dispute. At risk to their own safety, the officers enter the property to find the suspect upstairs, reportedly self-harming. There are reports he has strangled a dog. Across town, Tom and Nigel race to the scene of a serious crash that has left a pedestrian badly injured. As soon as they arrive, a scared and tearful woman approaches. She thinks the man lying in the middle of the road, being worked on by paramedics, is her partner. It’s down to Tom to deliver a message the woman will never forget. Later, adrenaline levels spike as Stephen and the team race to reports of an angry crowd surrounding the home of a convicted sex offender. As the tension rises, Stephen engages with the increasingly irate crowd, while Nigel formulates a plan to get the sex offender out of his house to safety.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: John Simpson, in discussion with the BBC's unparalleled range of experts across the world, analyses whether there has been a significant shift in the relationship between the United States and China, examines the long-term political turmoil in the United Kingdom, and looks at the psychological impact of Artificial Intelligence.

programme.title: The Turning
programme.desc: Supernatural thriller based on a story by Henry James. A young woman becomes the governess of two unruly children at a secluded manor.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Cute, funny, and sometimes surreal, AI-generated videos are flooding our social media feeds, but behind the dancing dogs and talking fruit lies a far darker world. In this Panorama investigation, reporter Marianna Spring explores how AI slop is shaping what we see, what we believe and who we can trust. From scam adverts and cloned voices to deepfake fraud, political manipulation and racially charged disinformation, the programme reveals how cheap and easily available AI tools are being weaponised. We ask, in the age of artificial intelligence, will seeing ever be believing again?

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: It’s the last round of heats. A new batch of amateur cooks enter the MasterChef kitchen ready to show off their culinary skills, hoping to get through to the next round.

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Hannah travels from California to Japan to experience the latest age-defying techniques and examine the true meaning of life.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: As the week edges towards its conclusion, Vernon Kay brings the quiz to Jedburgh. As ever, Paul Gorton is on tour-guiding duties, delivering fun facts, fibs and distractions along the way. TV icon Anneka Rice, comedian Shane Todd and Radio 1Xtra DJ Remi Burgz all have their sights set on the top of the leaderboard.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: We head to the Kent coast to revisit a renovation that's taken some time to finish. Jacqui checks out a large terrace as well as a tiny flat in a tale of two contrasting projects.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his Sardinian railway adventure, arriving in Mamoiada to investigate the town’s extraordinary pagan traditions. In a menacing mask, black sheepskin and 30 kg of cowbells, Michael embraces the role of a mamuthone, who wards off evil spirits. Taking a 1950s diesel railcar, Michael journeys across the island to Macomer, where he discovers the railway’s surprising connection to a pioneering Welsh railway engineer. His adventure concludes at Italy’s largest cork factory, where a team of expert sniffers safeguard the integrity of the world’s finest wines.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With the promise of warmer days ahead, Monty makes a start on planting up some of his spectacular summer containers. He’s also got leeks to plant out and shares some advice on how to make your own fertiliser. Frances Tophill heads to Berkshire to learn more about biodynamic gardening and the benefits it can bring. It’s also time for an update from Adam Frost’s new garden, where things are slowly starting to take shape. In Cumbria, a couple of growers share their love of auriculas as they get ready for this year’s RHS Chelsea Flower Show. And as part of Mental Health Awareness Week, a forager explains how her garden helped her to heal after being diagnosed with cancer.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards presents the quiz with a third dimension. In traditional quiz shows, questions have just right and wrong answers. But here, there is a twist - the Impossible answer. It's not just wrong, it couldn't possibly be right. Each day, 24 regular contestants compete for a possible £10,000 jackpot. Right answers keep them on track for the big money, but if at any stage they give an Impossible answer, that immediately knocks them out and they must try again the next day. Every show climaxes with the £10,000 question as the daily winner faces one last challenge. Three right answers trigger a spectacular cascade of 10,000 pound coins from a giant exclamation mark, but a single Impossible answer means they will leave with nothing.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents another heat of the classic quiz where contenders take on the famous black chair under the glare of the spotlight and the ticking clock in an attempt to become the next Mastermind champion. The specialist subjects are Christopher Hitchens, Ayrton Senna, Killing Eve and Field Marshal Montgomery.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

programme.title: Under the Vines
programme.desc: Sydney socialite Daisy Munroe and London lawyer Louis Oakley travel to New Zealand believing they are each sole heirs to a vineyard estate, only to discover that lawyer and vintner Vic Grimes’s definition of 'sole heirs' is flexible and that their idealistic notions of owning a vineyard, in reality, are less Tuscan romance and more dilapidated money pit. Finding themselves stuck with each other and a vineyard-sized problem to get rid off, they set about making a deal with influential locals Don and Marissa Silverton to sell it, but when the Peak View Wine Show brings a surprise for all, they wonder if the vineyard may not be as worthless as they first thought. With a change of heart and going home seemingly out of the question, will they make a go of the vineyard, or is disaster just around the corner?

programme.title: Wonders of Life
programme.desc: Brian travels across Australia to explore the physics of the size of life. He discovers that size has a profound effect on how you move, how you feed, even how long you live.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel presents the show in which experts invite members of the public to bring along their antiques for examination. Fascinating finds at St George's Hall in Liverpool include a light fitting from Hitler's Chancellery, a tie pin commemorating the death of Nelson, a picture frame so fine it needs no picture and the rarest of rare teapots.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Haddon Hall in Derbyshire, where he is joined by experts Michael Baggot and Caroline Hawley. Together the team pick out a selection of interesting antiques and collectibles to be sold at a local auction. Michael finds a glorious scent bottle, but will he come out of it smelling of roses? Caroline gets a glimpse into the past by looking through a stereoscopic viewer. Paul also takes the opportunity for a look around Haddon Hall, where he meets the current owner Lord Edward Manners and delves into the history of beer as it would have been brewed in medieval times at Haddon Hall, making the most of the opportunity by sampling a couple of brews.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Josh Jones, Molly Rainford, Mark Rhodes and Sonia take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo’s railway journey through the islands of Sardinia and Corsica sees him leave Sardinia and arrive into Corsica, the most mountainous island in the Mediterranean. In Ajaccio, he delves into the early life of the island’s most famous son, Napoleon Bonaparte, discovering how Corsica’s unique approach to childhood shaped the future conqueror. At a bustling food market, Michael samples the island’s wild herbs. He is captivated by the haunting harmonies of polyphonic singing, a sound deeply woven into the island’s soul. He then boards the legendary Trinichellu, Corsica’s historic metre-gauge mountain railway, for a breathtaking journey through the island’s rugged interior. In the heart of the wilderness, Michael discovers how cutting-edge technology and drones are being used in the battle against devastating wildfires, a crucial innovation protecting Corsica and beyond.

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: As milk production deadline day looms, Lucy and Adam race to finish the shed, buy more dairy cows and get them used to a new milking robot system. Callum and Zara have had a few difficult years since arriving on Arran, but their fortunes are at last turning as they build a future for their family. After a busy year, Andrew and Gayle hope their farming enterprises continue to grow and that grandad Terrence can take a step back.

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Monty Don, Arit Anderson and the team discover how gardens from the past are inspiring the latest looks at Chelsea this year. Frances Tophill explores the houseplant studios, Adam Frost has design ideas for garden seating, and Jamie Butterworth reveals this year’s colour trends. Monty celebrates the influence of Asian culture on our gardens, and Carol Klein explores the national plant collections in the Great Pavilion. Plus how you could help rescue some of Britain’s best-loved flowers.

programme.title: The Elon Musk Show
programme.desc: Another look at one of the world’s most polarising billionaires as he swaps rockets and electric cars for raw political power. The programme covers Musk’s tumultuous takeover of Twitter/X, the seismic fallouts of his personal life, and his evolution into a political force on both sides of the Atlantic. With immersive archive footage and revealing testimony from childhood friends, political insiders and former employees, this documentary charts how his quest to 'save humanity' collides with culture wars, AI breakthroughs and far-right movements in the US and UK.

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: Responding to reports of a brick being thrown through a window, Dave and Jade enter a run-down building in south Belfast. Dave quickly realises they are in a drug den, and the men inside are squatting. An addict with a feeding tube in his nose tells Stephen how injecting cocaine has brought him to death’s door. On patrol in the city centre, Adam revisits the scene of a suicide that haunts him to this day. Jade reveals the mental price of being a frontline cop. Meanwhile, in the early hours of the morning, as the clubs spill out onto the streets, the Peelers’ car is attacked. Perri uses pepper spray to bring the suspect down, but as he, Jade and Adam wrestle with the man, a large crowd surrounds them. For the first time in his career, Perri hits his emergency button.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Paddy O'Connell.

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A surgeon must remove part of his patient’s tongue and reconstruct it to save her from an aggressive cancer, and surgeons perform a rare simultaneous pancreas and kidney transplant.

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: When the bomb detonates inside Harvey’s Wagon Wheel Casino, it leaves behind more than shattered concrete and twisted steel. There's a maze of unanswered questions, until a lucky break leads to a psychological stand-off. Calculated risks unravel, loyalties facture, and the truth proves far more complicated than anyone imagined.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: A brand new batch of amateur cooks enter the MasterChef kitchen, ready to compete in the first round and take their shot at culinary stardom.

programme.title: The Cage
programme.desc: Matty and Leanne are pulled in different directions, and Gary's money laundering unravels at a tense casino night.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin is overcome by the mould at a Southampton semi. Owain loves the 70s period charm on offer in Herefordshire. And Jacqui is in upmarket Chelsea, but the flat she’s in is not as fashionable as the area... at least for now!

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo’s railway journey through the islands of Sardinia and Corsica sees him leave Sardinia and arrive into Corsica, the most mountainous island in the Mediterranean. In Ajaccio, he delves into the early life of the island’s most famous son, Napoleon Bonaparte, discovering how Corsica’s unique approach to childhood shaped the future conqueror. At a bustling food market, Michael samples the island’s wild herbs. He is captivated by the haunting harmonies of polyphonic singing, a sound deeply woven into the island’s soul. He then boards the legendary Trinichellu, Corsica’s historic metre-gauge mountain railway, for a breathtaking journey through the island’s rugged interior. In the heart of the wilderness, Michael discovers how cutting-edge technology and drones are being used in the battle against devastating wildfires, a crucial innovation protecting Corsica and beyond.

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: From a fragile silk robe and a wartime wall painting to a writer’s handmade timepiece, the hidden treasures in these two houses reveal the stories of two of Britain’s most celebrated authors – and the expert teams racing to preserve them.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Political Thinking with Nick Robinson
programme.desc: Nick Robinson in conversation with people who influence our political thinking.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards presents the quiz with a third dimension. In traditional quiz shows, questions have just right and wrong answers. But here, there is a twist - the Impossible answer. It's not just wrong, it couldn't possibly be right. Each day, 24 regular contestants compete for a possible £10,000 jackpot. Right answers keep them on track for the big money, but if at any stage they give an Impossible answer, that immediately knocks them out and they must try again tomorrow. Every show climaxes with the £10,000 question as the daily winner faces one last challenge. Three right answers trigger a spectacular cascade of 10,000 pound coins from a giant exclamation mark, but a single Impossible answer means they will leave with nothing.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie tests four more contenders on their specialist subjects and general knowledge in the tenth heat of the series. The specialist subjects are the life of Anne Lister, Dear Evan Hansen, Elizabeth I and the feature films of David Lynch.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

programme.title: Under the Vines
programme.desc: Daisy mucks in with Tippy and Gus, getting her hands dirty, while Louis, seeking advice on the value of Oakley, visits Hilary and gets more insight than he bargained for. Daisy learns about the Behind the Vines showcase from Nic, and Vic delivers some bad news to Marissa and Don. Meanwhile, Daisy and Louis hope to ease their financial woes with a visit to the bank but discover they may not be on the same page after all and that failing to comply with the 'law of the land' can have consequences. Daisy makes the ultimate sacrifice for the future of Oakley, giving the run-down vineyard a chance of survival. Or will it merely be a stay of execution?

programme.title: Wonders of Life
programme.desc: Brian Cox considers what it is about our world that makes it a home for life and asks what transformed this once barren planet into the earth we know today.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel presents the show in which members of the public are invited to bring along their antiques for examination. A Welsh dresser with no drawer handles, a travelling chamber stick used in the Crimean War and a sledge flag from Shackleton's ill-fated Antarctic trip of 1916 cause great excitement for the experts in Tenby.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Flog It! comes from the ancestral home of the Spencer family, Althorp in Northamptonshire. Nineteen generations have lived here, employing local people to work at the house and vast estate. Antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Will Axon find a selection of antiques and collectibles to take to auction. Christina is drawn to a 1950s toy pedal car, and Will finds a Mouseman tray. Paul Martin discovers how the Grand Union Canal was the waterway equivalent of the M1 as he takes to the water on a narrow boat and discovers what life was like for the boat people who ferried cargo from A to B.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Josh Jones, Molly Rainford, Mark Rhodes and Sonia take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo’s Mediterranean railway adventure reaches its finale in Corsica as he boards one of Europe’s most dramatic railways, which winds through the island’s breathtaking landscapes. Braving the heights, he takes a close-up look at the Pont du Vecchio, a spectacular viaduct along the route. In a forest of ancient chestnut trees, Michael discovers how this humble fruit is turned into an unexpected foodstuff which has sustained Corsicans for centuries. Michael learns how a by-product from the hives of the aggressive Corsican black bee is being used to create a new wonder product. His journey culminates in the stunning mountain town of Corte, where he uncovers an astonishing piece of British history and comes face to face with the resilient spirit of the Corsican people.

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Carol Klein and Diarmuid Gavin, along with guest judge and gardening fan Fred Sirieix, continue the search to find Northern Ireland’s greatest garden. First up is Anne’s Kilcootry Barn Gardens in Fintona, with two and a half acres of mixed gardens – filled with flower beds, a vegetable garden and an apple orchard. In Omagh, the judges visit a field of dreams – 17 acres of once-barren farmland that’s been transformed by Sir Bob and Lady Rosemary, with a formal Italianate area and a large woodland, planted with wildlife in mind. Finally, it’s off to the shores of Rossglass beach, overlooking Dundrum Bay, to visit artist Bernard’s romantic rose garden. Judges Diarmuid, Carol and Fred are searching for a garden which the owners have put their heart and soul into creating. Which will they decide deserves to be the last contender for the title of Greatest Garden?

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson and Brian Cuningham show how to help the pollinators in your garden and at Beechgrove as they focus on planting that will attract bees to your plot. Even if you don’t have a garden, Kirsty demonstrates how you can help the local ecosystem by sowing a meadow in a pot. She also discusses the best summer bulbs to plant now for fragrance and colour. Brian is back in the vegetable plot for his summer-long guide to productive gardening for beginners, and there is a demonstration of how to prune spring-flowering shrubs. All that and the week’s handy hints.

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: It’s the end of the week at the RHS Chelsea Flower Show 2026, and Monty Don and Arit Anderson are celebrating the great British summer as we all get ready to spend more time in our gardens. Adam Frost has plenty of design ideas for garden structures, and Frances Tophill reveals which new variety has been named Plant of the Year.

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: From scandalous society portraits to glittering royal furniture and the restoration of lost landscapes, this episode reveals how the legacy of the Grand Tour continues to shape Britain’s great houses – and the work being done today to protect their remarkable stories for the future.

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: At the forefront of its genre, the roll call of stand-ups who have performed in front of the famous Live At The Apollo lights plays out like a who’s who of comedic royalty, and this series is no different. Each episode will see a national (and sometimes international!) stand-up both compering and performing, before introducing two of the best established and up-coming stand-ups to the stage. This episode of Live at the Apollo is hosted by the fantastic Guz Khan as he introduces the fabulous Kiri Pritchard-McLean and the amazing Rhys James. Other acts to appear throughout the series include: Tom Allen, Rosie Jones, Kae Kurd, Sindhu Vee, Alex Edelman, Lou Sanders, Darren Harriott, Jessica Fostekew, Stephen Bailey, Desiree Burch, Paul McCaffrey, Jonny Pelham, Ardal O'Hanlon, Sara Barron, Mawaan Rizwan, Sarah Pascoe, Chris McCausland and Flo & Joan

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Matt Chorley.

programme.title: And Now for Something Completely Different
programme.desc: The first film version of classic sketches and memorable moments from the Monty Python gang's long-running television series, including Nudge Nudge, Hell's Grannies, Killer Cars, Dead Parrot, Lumberjack Song, Blackmail and Upper Class Twit of the Year.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Married at First Sight UK is one of Channel 4’s biggest shows, in which complete strangers marry in a mock-wedding and cameras follow what happens next. Panorama investigates allegations that raise serious concerns that welfare procedures on the reality show have failed contributors, leaving them at risk of harm. Noor Nanji speaks to three women who allege sexual misconduct by their former on-screen husbands. Two of the women say they were sexually assaulted. The men deny all the allegations against them, and both Channel 4 and CPL - the independent production company that makes the series - say the welfare protocols are robust and comprehensive. Following this Panorama investigation, Channel 4 has announced an external review into contributor welfare and has removed all previous seasons of MAFS UK.

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Tune every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: After the Farmer manages to drive his tractor into a mud patch, Shaun, Bitzer and the flock try to rescue it from the quagmire. Before long, however, the stranded vehicle sinks far beyond their reach.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: A late-night scary movie makes Penfold extra jittery, forcing Danger Mouse to make his sidekick sit out the latest mission to stop the Baron from flooding the entire world. When his search for Greenback proves fruitless, things go from bad to worse when Danger Mouse realises that the Baron has headed to the planet's only 100 per cent waterproof building and Penfold's current location - Danger HQ!

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes have found the Wishing Trident, which will help them on their quest to prevent the summoning of the legendary two-headed dragon, Iceburn. The problem is, Blossom has used one wish by mistake, and Herbert has accidentally used a second one. Now the heroes are left to defeat the dragon using only one wish, a never-ending ice cream cone (with sprinkles) and a giant spicy sausage hat!

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: When Dog is housebound with an injured paw, Mouse links a teddy bear to Dog's mind so that through the teddy, Dog can at least experience the great outdoors virtually.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy and the Lemmings happen upon a crying polar bear cub.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Sandy builds a ship in a bottle.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck and Frog work on a farm, Frog makes the perfect scarecrow. However, he gets covered in super-strong fertiliser and becomes a giant, monster-sized, chaos-causing Frog!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Frog tries to bring a touch of class to his new job in a cafe, but Duck's desire to wash each plate to polished perfection is holding him up.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: The Odd Squad investigates strange events. Oona must pass her lab director's test or risk getting kicked off Odd Squad.

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: Tricked into a town-wide clean-up, Dennis and Walter are paired up against their will. However, they are forced to cooperate to survive in the wilderness of Beanotown Woods.

programme.title: Bitesize Daily: 5-7 Year Olds
programme.desc: Lean about identifying instruments, organising instruments, and playing untuned percussion.

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: The brothers learn all about different types of cosy animal dens. Bird expert Nadeem helps Cam build a bird box, and Aaron reveals why the Asiatic lion is most endangered.

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: Captain Ratbeard takes to the high seas and sets sail on his maiden voyage, taking a look at everything from who not to kidnap if you’re a pirate to the dangers of pooing inside a submarine.

programme.title: Go Get Arty
programme.desc: Dragons, dinos and divas! The Kwapongs craft fire-breathers, Romesh Ranganathan draws a rapping T-rex, and Bristles dives into the bold, brilliant world of Frida Kahlo.

programme.title: Art Ninja
programme.desc: Ricky thinks he has nothing to spread on his toast, until a giant egg is mysteriously delivered. Worried it could contain a baby T rex, Ricky distracts himself with art, including a prehistoric mask and a paperweight inspired by Rachel Whiteread. At the park he uses his leftover toast to make a loaf-ly picture, but what will happen to the massive egg?

programme.title: Hey You What If?
programme.desc: Things don’t quite go to plan when the moon turns into cheese.

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Erin Ranney is a wildlife camera operator and adventurer. She splits her time between Alaska and the Falklands, and loves filming the planet’s most incredible wildlife. Erin’s mission is to film rockhopper penguins on the Falkland Islands and find out why these charismatic creatures are facing serious threats to their survival. She investigates why the rockhopper population has fallen by 80 per cent since the 1930s. Climate change is the biggest threat to the rockhoppers, causing violent storms that could see them face extinction. Erin also finds out how planting tussac grass can combat climate change and discovers that what we do in our own countries can affect these penguins thousands of miles away. She talks about how we can all make a difference towards helping the planet, just by taking small steps.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Gladiator Dynamite is back to make sure things don’t get too explosive when Dr Dan and Dr Raph get into a Body Battle in a planktastic challenge to test their core muscles. On the basketball court, we meet Joel Henry, our Brilliant Bodies slam-dunk champion, to find out how he jumps so much higher than Dr Chris. It’s game on in the lab when Dr Xand becomes Saliva Man and Dr Chris tries everything he knows to overcome his superpowers as the doctors show us what spit is for. In an amazing Bodytricks, six kids surprise themselves by lifting Dr Ronx off a chair using only two fingers! And in the emergency department in Edinburgh, we follow the treatment of Niamh, who has broken her elbow doing a back handspring at gymnastics, and Adhya, who took a whack on the thumb during a hockey match. Ouch!

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Sandro is a 14-year-old boy with a driving ambition to take part in the Le Mans 24-hour race. His big dream may be a few years off, but he is determined to make it happen. Sandro is a keen kart racer when we meet him, and we follow him as he pursues his dream to graduate to racing cars on professional circuits. We see him in kart-racing action, and are with him as he learns the enormity of what lies ahead if he is to achieve his dream of becoming a Formula One racing driver.

programme.title: Stage Stars
programme.desc: Senior dancer Jakob is hoping to compete in the world’s most prestigious ballet competition, but he has suffered an injury that could shatter his dreams. Meanwhile, the pressure is on for the Anything Goes cast, as rehearsals are hotting up and showtime is fast approaching – but there is always time for a classroom takeover!

programme.title: The Football Academy
programme.desc: Under-12s Carter and Alex jet off to Portugal for a huge tournament, Riley and Maddie take on Crystal Palace in the cup, and under-10 Julian takes on Spurs legend Sandro in The Maradona 7.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above & Beyond
programme.desc: Some baby longfin eels vanish, prompting the Octonauts to investigate the mystery.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Nugget arranges an ice-sculpting event, but only Flutter turns up. However, when his tongue gets accidentally stuck to the ice, it takes the whole colony to solve the problem.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck and Frog have to change a lighthouse’s light bulb, they quickly become marooned on the island. With no food, and surrounded by water, they must try to find a way to survive.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Only wood elves have ever defeated the mighty Eye of Vernon hat, so when the Riddlemaster wears the eye to take over the forest, the heroes quest to Wood Elf Wood to get help. However, Twinkle really doesn’t want to, as wood elves are so stuck up! Struggling to contain her irritation with their high-and-mighty ways, Twinkle still manages to offend them, and the heroes wonder how they will ever save the forest now.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall goes searching for Patagonia’s perfect predator: the puma. After waiting his whole life to see one, Steve sees a tiny cub and witnesses a female puma hunting an armadillo.

programme.title: Stage Stars
programme.desc: It's fireworks night! There’s a big display at school, and everyone is there. The Encore dancers set up a stall to raise money for costumes and have enough sweets and treats for everyone – but they need to sell them all. After months of rehearsals, it's finally opening night for Anything Goes - but will the cast hold their nerve and put on an outstanding performance?

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Children's animation. Set on a remote rural farm, Shaun's flock lives a peaceful life under the watchful eye of their sheepdog Bitzer. The sheep discover a mysterious buzzing object in their field. Shaun investigates and finds it's full of bees, who make the flock's life a misery.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Children's animation. Set on a remote rural farm, Shaun's flock leads a peaceful life under the watchful eye of their sheepdog, Bitzer, but Shaun's insatiable curiosity often leads him into the unlikeliest adventures. One dark and stormy night the flock are unable to sleep. Shaun investigates a series of sinister events only to discover who is really scaring the sheep.

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: Dennis and the gang convince Mrs Creecher that Monday is actually Sunday in the hope of extending their weekend.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Mr Friendlyface’s hat has been stolen by a Big Ole Worm, and the heroes must quest to get it back. As they trail the worm through the Prickly Desert, Trevor accidentally spikes himself all over on a cactus. But all is not lost – now that he is covered in sharp spikes, Trevor realises that he has a unique superpower, and when the time comes to confront the Big Ole Worm, it’s Spiky Trevor to the rescue!

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: When Lady Burlington's precious jewels are stolen, the kids believe that Cat was the unwitting thief and didn't know that she was stealing. They try to put things right by returning the valuables and teaching Cat the error of her ways. However, with Lady Burlington’s security guard searching for the burglar, the kids find their task way trickier than anticipated.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: A peace missionary panda tries to calm down Grizzy and the Lemmings during a fight.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings create a shadow puppet show where seven papercut lemmings confront a bear.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck is at the museum to give a really boring talk about dinosaurs. When Frog steps in, he somehow manages to hatch a baby dinosaur from an ancient egg.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck gets a job as a baker, Frog realises he can get free treats! However, a mix-up with the dough causes a birthday cake to grow out of control!

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob invents a new persona.

programme.title: Submarine Jim

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: On the town’s 100th-year anniversary party, Ozzie and Onom must stop Tracey Trifler from covering the town in trifle.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin poses as Speedy to sabotage his date with Starfire.

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons
programme.desc: The Rising Volt Tacklers reunite and prepare to take to the skies once more.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug & Cat Noir
programme.desc: Lady Noire and Mister Bug are back!

programme.title: Jamie Johnson FC
programme.desc: Ahead of Hawx’s home match against Foxborough, Madison makes a controversial decision which sends shock waves through the team. How will Charlie and Baz react?

programme.title: The Next Step

programme.desc: Gladiator Cyclone and Hacker T Dog show how to make a Gladiator's helmet.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: When two different pieces of flat-pack furniture are delivered to the Farmer and Bitzer, confusion ensues as they start to mix up the pieces. Shaun and the flock are forced to give them a lesson in following instructions.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: In the distant future, the social outcast Derek Mullard watches a video of the world being destroyed by history's biggest villain - Danger Mouse! To make matters worse, Danger Mouse is Derek's ancient ancestor and the reason he's forbidden from marrying his beautiful paramour, Pennyfold. Meanwhile, in the present day, Danger Mouse is in the midst of foiling Quark's latest plot, which involves auctioning off the nice bits of the world and compact crushing the rest. A time-travelling Derek arrives on the scene to confront Danger Mouse and prevent the world's destruction, correcting the course of history in the process.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Blossom draws the short cheese straw and, rather than join her friends on a picnic, she has to take a magic wand to the recycling centre. Faced with a long queue and a rumbly tummy, Blossom decides that life is too short for recycling - with disastrous consequences. The heroes must skip their picnic and go on a quest to save the forest from the magical mess Blossom has gotten them into.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: The gang is ready to celebrate Narwhal Day, one of their favourite holidays. However, they discover that it isn't a real holiday, just something Dad made up.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The magnetism of a meteorite close to the cabin inspires new games for the Lemmings.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: The long-lost episode of Mermaid Man is finally showing on TV.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Frog takes advantage of Duck's job at the observatory to watch a football game with the giant telescope - but first he has to sneak in and take control!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck must pick up leaves from the park, but Frog's arrival makes it seem impossible. That is, until Frog comes up with a clever solution - using springs from Duck’s beloved chair!

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: The Odd Squad investigates strange events. Olympia and Otis are forced to babysit Baby Genius.

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: The gang produce a hand-drawn comic which is a surprise hit all round Beanotown. Envious of their success, Walter embarks on a mission of cartoon-drawing sabotage.

programme.title: Bitesize Daily: 5-7 Year Olds
programme.desc: Learn about high- and low-pitched sounds, what pitch is, and what melody is.

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: The One Zoo Three brothers meet the zoo’s shyest animals as Cam is challenged to a game of spot the reptile, and Tyler tries to tempt Rambutan the binturong onto his new platform.

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: General strikes! Surplus women! Deadly mummy’s curses! Winston Churchill fangirling over Charlie Chaplin! Take a trip to the Roaring Twenties!

programme.title: Go Get Arty
programme.desc: Family portraits get personal! The Kwapongs try a clever painting hack, Tom Allen draws a super fancy unicorn, and Maisie Matilda paints a mini masterpiece on the edge of a book.

programme.title: Art Ninja
programme.desc: Ricky hears that a yeti has been spotted in Bristol, but there's no way it would be hiding in his flat, right? After making a camouflage picture inspired by Rousseau and a pinata shaped like an ice cream, Ricky's determined to lure the mysterious creature and be the first to photograph it. Heading to the woods with Michelle and Rich might be his only chance to capture the abominable beast.

programme.title: Hey You What If?
programme.desc: Find out why a flat Earth would feel very unflat indeed!

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Jahawi is an underwater camera operator who is passionate about preserving the coastline of Kenya, his home country. The ocean is home to a whole host of marine wildlife, but one species here is particularly under threat: dolphins. This fun and curious mammal is facing a number of issues, as their habitat is being destroyed and the fish they eat are being depleted. There is also significant human activity on this coastline, which is damaging the marine life. However, Lamu has stood the test of time and is a marine sanctuary for them, with a new port being built and things about to change. Working with the Kenyan Marine Mammal Network, Jahawi is hoping to get the area protected as a marine reserve before it’s too late. It doesn’t take long for Jahawi to find the resident bottle-nosed dolphins as they enjoy swimming at the bow of the boat, and he soon gets into the water with the dolphins to get a closer look at these magnificent creatures. One species of dolphin he is hoping to find and film is the rare humpback dolphin.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Dr Xand’s favourite cold virus is the rhinovirus: that’s one that gets right up your nose and gives you the sniffles. So when the doctors get a question in the lab asking why your nose runs when you have a cold, they decide to become rhinoviruses and attack the body’s defences through a giant nose. We also get a chance to see right inside Dr Xand’s nostrils when he sticks a camera up his nose to show Dr Chris a real-life, flapping bogey specimen - gross alert! Dr Ronx makes a whole class of schoolchildren forget where their elbows are with a mind-blowing body trick you can try on your friends, and in Ouch and About, Ethan wants to know which is the strongest bone in his body. Over in Australia, six-year-old Jakobi is flown by helicopter to Sydney by the Flying Doctors because his local hospital thinks he needs specialist care. Lucy goes to the hospital in Edinburgh to find out why she’s woken up with a swollen face. And in Liverpool, Michael’s foot has been run over by a car, so no surprise that he’s ended up in the emergency department. Ouch!

With fast-swimming prey like tuna and mackerel, mako sharks need to move quickly. But how do they reach such meteoric speeds? Steve first snorkels with these lightning-quick sharks off the coast of San Diego in California, before getting under their skin in his shark lab to reveal their hidden speed secrets.

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: An enormous pillow fight has broken out in the sewer between Rattus and his siblings. With that in mind, he brings us a special battle-themed show, chock-full of fight-filled history. It includes the English Armada, William the Conqueror getting schooled by a rival’s mum, invading Romans and scary Spartans, state-of-the-art weapons and warring pigeons. Loo Man proves to be an unwelcome visitor in a submarine, and loving mum Lady Harley writes a letter to her son while her home is under siege during the English Civil War!

programme.title: Go Get Arty

programme.title: Art Ninja
programme.desc: Ricky is having really weird dreams, which lead to him to make surreal art including giant hands and a burger hat inspired by Rene Magritte. After scoffing some cheesy snacks, Ricky and Gavin both fall asleep and Ricky gets his pyjamas on to create some illuminating dream art.

programme.title: Hey You What If?
programme.desc: The planet is being destroyed - but this episode demonstrates the things you can do to help save it.

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Megan is a wildlife expert who campaigns for animal rights and has a passion for shark conservation. Her mission is to look into the threats facing sharks in British waters and around the globe, from the deadly fin trade to industrial fishing, which have caused shark population numbers to fall dramatically in recent decades. One species she is keen to know more about is the blue shark, millions of which are caught each year for their fins, which can fetch thousands of dollars in Asia and have also ended up in Chinese restaurants in the UK. Megan also investigates the story behind a recent report that one of the nation’s favourite takeaways – fish and chips – has been linked to a species of shark which is listed as globally vulnerable and is on the brink of extinction in British waters. Megan meets up with marine biologists and shark conservationists to find out more about the dangers to British sharks and explores ways in which sharks and other fish species can be protected. As part of her mission, she heads to the Cornish coast and dons her snorkelling gear to try and get a glimpse of the beautiful blue shark in its natural habitat.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: How does the body know when it needs to wee? The doctors find out in the lab! An optical illusion turns Dr Xand’s face green in Mindbenders, and it's time to check in on the Ouchers.

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Laleeyah lives on the Crow Creek Native American reservation in South Dakota, USA. There aren’t many opportunities for people on the reservation, and life can be tough, but Laleeyah loves to learn about the traditions of her ancestors and takes pride in keeping them alive. Once a year there is the Pow Wow: a big, colourful, celebration where the whole Crow Creek tribe comes together to compete and celebrate with traditional dancing, music and dress. For several years running Laleeyah has been crowned Junior Miss Crow Creek - the best young dancer on the reservation. But since the last Pow Wow she has developed an illness called hyperthyroidism, which makes her feel weak and tired. As she comes to terms with her illness, Laleeyah still wants to hold on to the things she loves most: dancing, representing her tribe, and her crown. But will she have what it takes, and can she still be the dancing queen?

programme.title: Stage Stars

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: Southern Africa is a riot of life and colour because of two great ocean currents that sweep around the continent's Cape. To the east, the warm Agulhas current, generating clouds that roll inland to the wettest place in southern Africa. To the west is the cold Benguela current, home to more great white sharks than anywhere else. Moisture-laden fog rolls inland, supporting wonderful desert garden. And where the two currents meet, the clash of warm and cold water creates one of the world's most fabulous natural spectacles: South Africa's sardine run. This is the greatest gathering of predators on the planet, including Africa's largest, the brydes whale.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
